recommendations: add and run an MCP-specific test suite in CI (smoke tests for all exported endpoints plus regression tests for fit_variogram/SRF/kriging flows)
pin and constrain plugin/runtime dependencies in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` (and mirror in a lockfile) to improve reproducibility
add strict input/output validation with clear error messages for every endpoint (array shapes
dimensionality
anisotropy/rotation params
optional dependency checks)
improve API schema/docs in `README_MCP.md` with per-endpoint examples and expected units/defaults
implement structured logging and optional telemetry hooks around endpoint calls for easier debugging/performance tracking
introduce caching or memoization for repeated expensive model setup/fitting paths to reduce latency
add benchmark scripts for key workloads (variogram estimation
kriging
SRF generation) and track performance budgets in CI
strengthen numerical robustness tests (edge cases
degenerate covariance params
near-singular kriging systems
NaN/inf handling)
standardize naming/alias policy between GSTools classes and MCP endpoints (e.g.
`CondSRF` vs `condsrf`) and document compatibility guarantees
add versioned MCP API contracts and changelog entries for breaking/non-breaking endpoint changes
expand interoperability tests for optional exports (`to_vtk`
`to_pyvista`
`to_structured`) when optional deps are missing/present
enforce code quality gates on generated plugin code (ruff/black/mypy + minimal type coverage threshold)
provide async/concurrency safety guidance and tests if endpoints are used in parallel workloads
add a small set of high-value end-to-end examples (fit variogram → model selection → kriging/conditioned field → export) to validate real user journeys

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all expected nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
High import feasibility (0.95) and low intrusiveness risk enabled adapter_mode=import, which is generally the safest and fastest integration path.
Repository has clean Python packaging metadata via pyproject.toml and a well-structured src layout, improving automated discovery.
Generated MCP service started healthy (stdio transport), indicating basic runtime viability of the plugin scaffold.
No runtime errors or warnings were emitted by the pipeline.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing depth of automated architectural/context enrichment.
Original project test result is marked passed=false with execution_time=0 and empty details, indicating tests were likely not executed rather than failing assertions.

node_performance.download_time: Repository ingestion succeeded via zip_fallback; likely efficient for this project size (139 files), but exact node timing unavailable.
node_performance.analysis_time: Static/module analysis appears complete and high quality (core module mapping + endpoint candidates), with one non-blocking DeepWiki failure.
node_performance.generation_time: Code generation completed quickly within total runtime (~242s), producing full MCP scaffold and dependency manifest.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed, but substantive endpoint/functional tests are missing; original test suite appears unexecuted.
